name: Avoid Bad Comments
source: Clean Code by Robert C. Martin, Chapter 4: Comments
problem: Noisy, redundant, misleading, mandated, or commented-out code clutters the codebase and erodes trust in all comments, including accurate ones, while distracting readers from the actual logic.
steps: Remove any comment that merely restates what the code already says (redundant comment).
Never check in commented-out code; use version control to retain history instead.
Delete position markers and banner comments that add no information.
Remove mandated Javadoc on every function when many of them contain nothing but noise.
Fix misleading comments that claim the code does something other than what it actually does.
